mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in >60% of triggered cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >50% of periods where industrial production grows, or the signal shows zero predictive power during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeds 2.5%, markets price in imminent monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns are positive in >65% of months where CPI inflation breaches the 2.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized indicates sustained economic expansion and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >50% of quarters where GDP growth exceeds 2.0%, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening supply-side momentum, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 generates positive returns in >60% of periods following a negative industrial production reading, invalidating the contraction signal.
